headline: Neonatal Mortality Rate Stagnates at 4.8 Per 1,000 Live Births
context: Progress in reducing newborn deaths has plateaued since 2018 with premature birth complications as the leading cause.

cards.big: Neonatal mortality stagnant at 4.8 per 1,000 live births since 2018 no improvement in 7 years
cards.sub: Premature birth and low birth weight account for 55% of newborn deaths

cards.big: Only 52% of district hospitals have functioning neonatal intensive care units
cards.sub: Babies born premature at hospitals without NICU face 3 times higher mortality

cards.big: Neonatal transport service covers only 65% of the country with average response of 2.8 hours
cards.sub: Critical hours lost in transport significantly reduce survival chances for sick newborns

cards.big: Sri Lanka achieved neonatal mortality of 3.6 per 1,000 through universal midwife-led newborn care
cards.sub: Community-level skilled birth attendance prevents complications before they become fatal

cards.big: Stagnant newborn survival rates in a growing economy signal that resources are going to wrong priorities
Should every district hospital have a minimum-standard neonatal care unit within 3 years
